agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes across five stakeholder groups.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a subdomain of Order Fulfillment covering express-eligible order placement, express driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and express-specific refund processing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a 2-hour delivery window, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse, fulfilled by drivers with a minimum 4-star rating."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge applied to express deliveries, separate from the base delivery fee."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the delivery must be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window from order placement within which the driver must pick up the express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Eligible","definition":"An order qualifies for express when the delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Qualified Driver","definition":"A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above, eligible to fulfill express deliveries."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A refund of the express fee (not base delivery fee) issued when the express window is missed — framed by Legal as a discretionary gesture, not a contractual obligation."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge that is never refunded under the express refund policy."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — express timelines are impossible beyond this radius. Offering express outside this range would guarantee failure.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a delivery address >10km from the nearest warehouse, WHEN they attempt to select express delivery, THEN the express option must not be available."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations states the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes — this is a hard operational constraint that gates the entire SLA.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at time T, WHEN no driver picks up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (exact escalation path TBD — see escalations)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-based threshold that operations could adjust. A VP could lower this to 3.5 if driver supply is constrained.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars, WHEN the system considers them for an express order, THEN they must be excluded from the candidate pool."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that Finance can adjust.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selecting express delivery, WHEN the order is priced, THEN $12.99 must be added as a separate express fee line item."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the express window is missed, only the express fee ($12.99) is refunded — never the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision that could change.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is issued, THEN the refund amount must equal exactly the express fee and the base delivery fee must remain charged."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the express window is missed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy — Finance could change the exception criteria.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery that missed the window AND the delivery address was flagged as customer-error, WHEN the refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund must be denied."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is an operational SLA that Finance could adjust.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a refund approved for a missed express window, WHEN the refund is initiated, THEN it must be processed within 3 business days."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund is triggered.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-based escalation rule. The 10-minute threshold and automatic refund are business decisions.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery where the assigned driver cancels, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver accepting, THEN the express fee must be automatically refunded."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Express customers must be able to track their driver's location in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an active express delivery, WHEN the customer views the order, THEN the driver's current location must be displayed and updated in real time."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is shown with the $12.99 fee and the 2-hour window prominently displayed"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer whose delivery address is more than 10km from any warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is not shown or is shown as unavailable"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"No driver picks up within 15 minutes","then":"The system escalates per the defined escalation path (exact behavior TBD pending contradiction resolution)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window and the address was correct","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"The express fee of $12.99 is refunded; the base delivery fee is not refunded"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery misses the 2-hour window but the customer provided an incorrect address","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"No refund is issued"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"No replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The express fee is automatically refunded without manual intervention"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express delivery is in progress","when":"The customer views their order","then":"The driver's real-time location is displayed on a map"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An express order refund has been approved","when":"The refund is processed","then":"The refund is completed within 3 business days"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver picks up at minute 14 but delivery completes at 2 hours 5 minutes","expected_behavior":"Pickup SLA was met, but express window was missed. Standard missed-window refund policy applies (refund express fee if address was correct)."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address mid-delivery to a location >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should the express guarantee be voided? Should the order convert to standard? Needs product decision."},{"scenario":"No express-qualified drivers (4+ stars) are available at order time","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should express option be hidden dynamically based on driver availability, or shown with a warning? Risk of accepting orders that cannot be fulfilled."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 119 of the 2-hour window","expected_behavior":"Both the 10-minute replacement window (BR-8) and the 2-hour express window will almost certainly be missed. Automatic refund triggers from the replacement rule before the window-miss rule."},{"scenario":"Customer disputes that they gave a wrong address","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Who adjudicates? What evidence is required to classify an address as 'customer-error'? Needs a dispute resolution process."},{"scenario":"Warehouse is temporarily closed or out of stock","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should express eligibility be dynamic based on warehouse operational status?"},{"scenario":"Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one is used for distance calculation?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Nearest warehouse? Any warehouse? Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Refund approved on a Friday — does the 3-business-day clock start immediately or next business day?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Finance needs to clarify whether the approval day counts as day 1."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels, replacement is assigned at minute 9, but replacement also cancels","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Does the 10-minute clock reset? Is there a cap on replacement attempts?"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"$12.99 express fee represents incremental revenue per order. Refund exposure is capped at the express fee per order. Automatic refund on replacement failure (BR-8) could create significant refund volume if driver supply is tight. Wrong-address exclusion (BR-6) reduces refund leakage.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA creates pressure on driver dispatch systems. 4-star driver requirement constrains the eligible driver pool. 10-minute replacement window requires robust real-time driver reassignment. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure and data pipeline.","user":"Premium experience with real-time tracking and fast delivery. However, the contradiction between 'guarantee' language shown to customers and 'best effort' legal framing creates trust risk if customers feel misled.","risk":"PRIMARY RISK: Showing '2-hour guarantee' to customers while Legal says it cannot be a guarantee creates legal exposure and customer trust issues. If customers screenshot the guarantee language and the delivery is late, the company is in a weak position regardless of legal framing. Secondary risk: tight operational SLAs (15min pickup, 10min replacement) with insufficient driver supply could cause cascading failures and high refund rates.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Expect a guaranteed 2-hour delivery based on prominent UI messaging. If this is actually best-effort, perceived value of $12.99 fee may not match expectations. Trust damage if windows are frequently missed."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star rating requirement excludes a segment of the driver pool. Express orders create time pressure. Cancellation triggers replacement cascade."},{"group":"Operations","impact":"Must maintain 15-minute pickup SLA and 10-minute replacement SLA. Needs monitoring and alerting infrastructure for express-specific metrics."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Must process refunds within 3 business days. Needs reporting on express refund rates to monitor margin impact. Automatic refunds (BR-8) bypass manual approval."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Directly affected by the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction. Must review all customer-facing language before launch."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle complaints when express windows are missed. Needs clear scripts for guarantee vs best-effort framing. Real-time tracking reduces inbound 'where is my order' contacts."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The core value proposition — whether this is a 'guarantee' or 'best effort' — is directly contradicted between stakeholders. PM and Customer Success explicitly use the word 'guaranteed' while Legal explicitly prohibits guaranteeing delivery time in writing. This is not a semantic nuance; it fundamentally changes the product's promise, the refund policy's nature, and all customer-facing language. Until this is resolved, the domain model cannot be finalized.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99) and its separation from base fee","10km warehouse proximity eligibility rule","4-star minimum driver rating requirement","15-minute pickup SLA","Refund excludes base delivery fee","Wrong-address refund exclusion"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens when 15-minute pickup SLA is breached — no escalation path defined","How 'wrong address' is determined and who adjudicates disputes","Whether express availability should be dynamic based on driver supply","How the 10-minute replacement window interacts with multiple consecutive cancellations","Whether the 3-business-day refund clock includes or excludes the approval day"],"assumptions":["The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance","Driver rating is a rolling average, not a per-delivery score","The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup","Automatic refund (BR-8) follows the same 3-business-day processing timeline as manual refunds"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM states express deliveries are 'guaranteed within 2 hours' and Customer Success says the '2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states 'We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing' and frames the refund as a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions directly conflict. The product cannot simultaneously present a guarantee to customers and disclaim it legally. This affects every customer-facing touchpoint: UI copy, marketing, terms of service, refund policy framing, and customer support scripts.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A: Use 'guarantee' language publicly and accept legal risk, relying on goodwill refunds to mitigate complaints","B: Use 'best effort' or 'aim' language (e.g., 'We aim to deliver within 2 hours') with refund as a satisfaction policy, not a guarantee fulfillment","C: Use 'guarantee' language but back it with contractual Terms of Service that formalize the refund as a binding commitment — requires Legal to agree"],"recommendation":"Option B. Use aspirational language ('We aim to deliver within 2 hours — if we don't, we'll refund the express fee') that communicates the intent without creating a contractual guarantee. This satisfies Legal's constraint while preserving customer confidence. Legal, PM, and Customer Success must align on exact wording before any UI work begins."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success states that if a replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes, 'we refund automatically.' This creates an automatic, system-triggered refund path that bypasses Finance's stated 3-business-day processing window and potentially conflicts with Finance's wrong-address exclusion (BR-6). If a driver cancels on a wrong-address delivery and no replacement is found, does the automatic refund fire even though the address was wrong? The automatic trigger and the manual exception criteria are in tension.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Finance","Operations"],"options":["A: Automatic refund fires unconditionally on replacement timeout, regardless of address validity — simple but creates refund leakage","B: Automatic refund is subject to the same wrong-address exclusion — requires real-time address validation before the 10-minute timer fires","C: Automatic refund fires immediately but can be clawed back within the 3-business-day window if wrong-address is confirmed"],"recommendation":"Option B. The wrong-address exclusion should apply universally to all refund triggers, including automatic ones. However, this requires that address validation is resolved before or during the 10-minute replacement window, which adds operational complexity. Product and Finance must decide."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes across five stakeholder groups.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a subdomain of Order Fulfillment covering express-eligible order placement, express driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and express-specific re

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for contradictions, ambiguities, and gaps before producing my JSON output.

Key contradiction: PM says "guaranteed within 2 hours" and Customer Success says "2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently" — but Legal says "We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing" and it's "best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction between stakeholders.

Let me identify all issues:

1. CONTRADICTION: PM/CS say "guaranteed" vs Legal says "cannot guarantee in writing" — this is the core conflict
2. The refund: PM says "refund express fee if we miss window", Finance says "refund processed within 3 business days", Legal says "refund is goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable" — these aren't contradictory per se, but the framing differs


3. Customer Success introduces an automatic refund trigger if replacement isn't assigned within 10 minutes, which nobody else mentioned — that's a new policy constraint I need to flag

Since there's a direct contradiction between guaranteed delivery and best-effort language, this warrants escalation with low confidence given the conflicting stakeholder positions.

trace.result: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a time-window commitment, premium pricing, real-time tracking, and a refund policy for missed windows. Synthesized from raw product discovery workshop notes across five stakeholder groups.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a subdomain of Order Fulfillment covering express-eligible order placement, express driver assignment, time-window enforcement, real-time tracking, and express-specific refund processing.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option with a 2-hour delivery window, available to customers within 10km of a warehouse, fulfilled by drivers with a minimum 4-star rating."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"The $12.99 surcharge applied to express deliveries, separate from the base delivery fee."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the delivery must be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window from order placement within which the driver must pick up the express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Eligible","definition":"An order qualifies for express when the delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse."},{"term":"Express-Qualified Driver","definition":"A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above, eligible to fulfill express deliveries."},{"term":"Replacement Window","definition":"The 10-minute window to assign a replacement driver after a mid-delivery cancellation before an automatic refund is triggered."},{"term":"Goodwill Refund","definition":"A refund of the express fee (not base delivery fee) issued when the express window is missed — framed by Legal as a discretionary gesture, not a contractual obligation."},{"term":"Base Delivery Fee","definition":"The standard delivery charge that is never refunded under the express refund policy."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — express timelines are impossible beyond this radius. Offering express outside this range would guarantee failure.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a delivery address >10km from the nearest warehouse, WHEN they attempt to select express delivery, THEN the express option must not be available."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Operations states the 2-hour window cannot be met if pickup exceeds 15 minutes — this is a hard operational constraint that gates the entire SLA.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at time T, WHEN no driver picks up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (exact escalation path TBD — see escalations)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-based threshold that operations could adjust. A VP could lower this to 3.5 if driver supply is constrained.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars, WHEN the system considers them for an express order, THEN they must be excluded from the candidate pool."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing is a business policy that Finance can adjust.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selecting express delivery, WHEN the order is priced, THEN $12.99 must be added as a separate express fee line item."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When the express window is missed, only the express fee ($12.99) is refunded — never the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund scope is a business policy decision that could change.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery completed after the 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is issued, THEN the refund amount must equal exactly the express fee and the base delivery fee must remain charged."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, even if the express window is missed.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy — Finance could change the exception criteria.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery that missed the window AND the delivery address was flagged as customer-error, WHEN the refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund must be denied."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Processing timeline is an operational SLA that Finance could adjust.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a refund approved for a missed express window, WHEN the refund is initiated, THEN it must be processed within 3 business days."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If a driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes or an automatic refund is triggered.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Policy-based escalation rule. The 10-minute threshold and automatic refund are business decisions.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery where the assigned driver cancels, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver accepting, THEN the express fee must be automatically refunded."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Express customers must be able to track their driver's location in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an active express delivery, WHEN the customer views the order, THEN the driver's current location must be displayed and updated in real time."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is shown with the $12.99 fee and the 2-hour window prominently displayed"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer whose delivery address is more than 10km from any warehouse","when":"They view delivery options","then":"The express delivery option is not shown or is shown as unavailable"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"No driver picks up within 15 minutes","then":"The system escalates per the defined escalation path (exact behavior TBD pending contradiction resolution)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express delivery is completed after the 2-hour window and the address was correct","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"The express fee of $12.99 is refunded; the base delivery fee is not refunded"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express delivery misses the 2-hour window but the customer provided an incorrect address","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"No refund is issued"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"No replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The express fee is automatically refunded without manual intervention"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express delivery is in progress","when":"The customer views their order","then":"The driver's real-time location is displayed on a map"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An express order refund has been approved","when":"The refund is processed","then":"The refund is completed within 3 business days"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Driver picks up at minute 14 but delivery completes at 2 hours 5 minutes","expected_behavior":"Pickup SLA was met, but express window was missed. Standard missed-window refund policy applies (refund express fee if address was correct)."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address mid-delivery to a location >10km from warehouse","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should the express guarantee be voided? Should the order convert to standard? Needs product decision."},{"scenario":"No express-qualified drivers (4+ stars) are available at order time","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should express option be hidden dynamically based on driver availability, or shown with a warning? Risk of accepting orders that cannot be fulfilled."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels at minute 119 of the 2-hour window","expected_behavior":"Both the 10-minute replacement window (BR-8) and the 2-hour express window will almost certainly be missed. Automatic refund triggers from the replacement rule before the window-miss rule."},{"scenario":"Customer disputes that they gave a wrong address","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Who adjudicates? What evidence is required to classify an address as 'customer-error'? Needs a dispute resolution process."},{"scenario":"Warehouse is temporarily closed or out of stock","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Should express eligibility be dynamic based on warehouse operational status?"},{"scenario":"Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one is used for distance calculation?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Nearest warehouse? Any warehouse? Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Refund approved on a Friday — does the 3-business-day clock start immediately or next business day?","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Finance needs to clarify whether the approval day counts as day 1."},{"scenario":"Driver cancels, replacement is assigned at minute 9, but replacement also cancels","expected_behavior":"Undefined. Does the 10-minute clock reset? Is there a cap on replacement attempts?"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"$12.99 express fee represents incremental revenue per order. Refund exposure is capped at the express fee per order. Automatic refund on replacement failure (BR-8) could create significant refund volume if driver supply is tight. Wrong-address exclusion (BR-6) reduces refund leakage.","operational":"15-minute pickup SLA creates pressure on driver dispatch systems. 4-star driver requirement constrains the eligible driver pool. 10-minute replacement window requires robust real-time driver reassignment. Real-time tracking requires GPS infrastructure and data pipeline.","user":"Premium experience with real-time tracking and fast delivery. However, the contradiction between 'guarantee' language shown to customers and 'best effort' legal framing creates trust risk if customers feel misled.","risk":"PRIMARY RISK: Showing '2-hour guarantee' to customers while Legal says it cannot be a guarantee creates legal exposure and customer trust issues. If customers screenshot the guarantee language and the delivery is late, the company is in a weak position regardless of legal framing. Secondary risk: tight operational SLAs (15min pickup, 10min replacement) with insufficient driver supply could cause cascading failures and high refund rates.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Expect a guaranteed 2-hour delivery based on prominent UI messaging. If this is actually best-effort, perceived value of $12.99 fee may not match expectations. Trust damage if windows are frequently missed."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star rating requirement excludes a segment of the driver pool. Express orders create time pressure. Cancellation triggers replacement cascade."},{"group":"Operations","impact":"Must maintain 15-minute pickup SLA and 10-minute replacement SLA. Needs monitoring and alerting infrastructure for express-specific metrics."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Must process refunds within 3 business days. Needs reporting on express refund rates to monitor margin impact. Automatic refunds (BR-8) bypass manual approval."},{"group":"Legal / Compliance","impact":"Directly affected by the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction. Must review all customer-facing language before launch."},{"group":"Customer Success","impact":"Will handle complaints when express windows are missed. Needs clear scripts for guarantee vs best-effort framing. Real-time tracking reduces inbound 'where is my order' contacts."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The core value proposition — whether this is a 'guarantee' or 'best effort' — is directly contradicted between stakeholders. PM and Customer Success explicitly use the word 'guaranteed' while Legal explicitly prohibits guaranteeing delivery time in writing. This is not a semantic nuance; it fundamentally changes the product's promise, the refund policy's nature, and all customer-facing language. Until this is resolved, the domain model cannot be finalized.","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99) and its separation from base fee","10km warehouse proximity eligibility rule","4-star minimum driver rating requirement","15-minute pickup SLA","Refund excludes base delivery fee","Wrong-address refund exclusion"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens when 15-minute pickup SLA is breached — no escalation path defined","How 'wrong address' is determined and who adjudicates disputes","Whether express availability should be dynamic based on driver supply","How the 10-minute replacement window interacts with multiple consecutive cancellations","Whether the 3-business-day refund clock includes or excludes the approval day"],"assumptions":["The 10km distance is calculated as straight-line (geodesic) distance, not driving distance","Driver rating is a rolling average, not a per-delivery score","The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup","Automatic refund (BR-8) follows the same 3-business-day processing timeline as manual refunds"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"PM states express deliveries are 'guaranteed within 2 hours' and Customer Success says the '2-hour guarantee should be shown prominently.' Legal explicitly states 'We cannot guarantee a 2-hour delivery time in writing' and frames the refund as a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions directly conflict. The product cannot simultaneously present a guarantee to customers and disclaim it legally. This affects every customer-facing touchpoint: UI copy, marketing, terms of service, refund policy framing, and customer support scripts.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Customers","Marketing"],"options":["A: Use 'guarantee' language publicly and accept legal risk, relying on goodwill refunds to mitigate complaints","B: Use 'best effort' or 'aim' language (e.g., 'We aim to deliver within 2 hours') with refund as a satisfaction policy, not a guarantee fulfillment","C: Use 'guarantee' language but back it with contractual Terms of Service that formalize the refund as a binding commitment — requires Legal to agree"],"recommendation":"Option B. Use aspirational language ('We aim to deliver within 2 hours — if we don't, we'll refund the express fee') that communicates the intent without creating a contractual guarantee. This satisfies Legal's constraint while preserving customer confidence. Legal, PM, and Customer Success must align on exact wording before any UI work begins."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success states that if a replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes, 'we refund automatically.' This creates an automatic, system-triggered refund path that bypasses Finance's stated 3-business-day processing window and potentially conflicts with Finance's wrong-address exclusion (BR-6). If a driver cancels on a wrong-address delivery and no replacement is found, does the automatic refund fire even though the address was wrong? The automatic trigger and the manual exception criteria are in tension.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Finance","Operations"],"options":["A: Automatic refund fires unconditionally on replacement timeout, regardless of address validity — simple but creates refund leakage","B: Automatic refund is subject to the same wrong-address exclusion — requires real-time address validation before the 10-minute timer fires","C: Automatic refund fires immediately but can be clawed back within the 3-business-day window if wrong-address is confirmed"],"recommendation":"Option B. The wrong-address exclusion should apply universally to all refund triggers, including automatic ones. However, this requires that address validation is resolved before or during the 10-minute replacement window, which adds operational complexity. Product and Finance must decide."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
